notes: Section 286, R.S. § 4881; , , provided penalty for defacing cemeteries. See , Veterans’ Benefits.act Mar. 3, 1911, ch. 231, § 29136 Stat. 1167section 901 of Title 38
Section 287, R.S. § 4882, related to jurisdiction of United States upon purchase of any national cemetery with consent of any State legislature.

Section 288, acts , title II, ; , title II, ; , title II, ; , title II, ; , title II, ; , ; , title III, ; , title III, , prohibited expenditure of any appropriation for maintenance of more than a single approach to any national cemetery.Feb. 12, 1925, ch. 22543 Stat. 926Apr. 15, 1926, ch. 14644 Stat. 287Feb. 23, 1927, ch. 16744 Stat. 1138Mar. 23, 1928, ch. 23245 Stat. 354Feb. 28, 1929, ch. 36645 Stat. 1375June 30, 1954, ch. 425, § 10168 Stat. 331July 15, 1955, ch. 37069 Stat. 360July 2, 1956, ch. 49070 Stat. 479
Section 289, , , provided for conveyance to State or municipality of approach road to national cemetery. See , Veterans’ Benefits.act May 23, 1941, ch. 13055 Stat. 191section 2404(f) of Title 38
Section 290, , , prohibited encroachment by railroad on rights of way. See .act July 27, 1953, ch. 245, § 10167 Stat. 197section 2404 of Title 38

Effective Date of RepealRepeal effective , or such earlier date as the President may prescribe and publish in the Federal Register, see , set out as a note under , Veterans’ Benefits.Sept. 1, 1973section 10(c) of Pub. L. 93–43section 2306 of Title 38
